Reconstructing whether the thesis is coherent requires resolving this conceptual mismatch.","R1.2":"Multiple logical fractures are present: (1) the method is labeled MPC but implemented as a reactive threshold rule, so the framing and mechanism are inconsistent; (2) the ablation removes the depth-based safety check—a reactive component—yet is used to justify the value of model-predictive reasoning, making the inference circular; (3) the claim of 'same collision-avoidance rate' as the baseline coexists with three failures, which requires scrutiny. Logical consistency is load-bearing for any credible check of this artifact.","R1.3":"The central empirical claim—that model-predictive reasoning yields smoother interaction—is unsupported: the 23% stop-reduction result does not isolate the predictive component, and the ablation tests the wrong module to attribute the effect to MPC. Evidence does not align with the contribution claim."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should I perform a technical/logical review (consistency of claims, methodology, and evidence) or a writing-quality review (prose clarity, grammar, and style)?"}

rationale.R1.1: The paper's core framing ('model-predictive') directly conflicts with the control loop described, which is purely reactive (distance threshold → decelerate, else follow nominal controller); no future-trajectory reasoning is operationalized. Reconstructing whether the thesis is coherent requires resolving this conceptual mismatch.
rationale.R1.2: Multiple logical fractures are present: (1) the method is labeled MPC but implemented as a reactive threshold rule, so the framing and mechanism are inconsistent; (2) the ablation removes the depth-based safety check—a reactive component—yet is used to justify the value of model-predictive reasoning, making the inference circular; (3) the claim of 'same collision-avoidance rate' as the baseline coexists with three failures, which requires scrutiny. Logical consistency is load-bearing for any credible check of this artifact.
rationale.R1.3: The central empirical claim—that model-predictive reasoning yields smoother interaction—is unsupported: the 23% stop-reduction result does not isolate the predictive component, and the ablation tests the wrong module to attribute the effect to MPC. Evidence does not align with the contribution claim.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I perform a technical/logical review (consistency of claims, methodology, and evidence) or a writing-quality review (prose clarity, grammar, and style)?
